title: Willughbeia beccarii
intro: Willughbeia beccarii is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, native to the island of New Guinea. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of W. beccarii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, W. beccarii is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin problems.
